title: Other elementary services occupations n.e.c.
description: All artists and related workers not listed separately. Perform dances. May perform on stage, for broadcasting, or for video recording. Create new dance routines. Rehearse performance of routines. May direct and stage presentations. (Based on 64 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Manage dance schools or assist in their management, teaching students and dancers about rhythm, interpretive movement, and assessing their dancing abilities to determine areas for improvement.
Record dance movements and their technical aspects, using a technical understanding of choreography, while developing new dance ideas, keeping notes and sketches to document influences.

Direct rehearsals and stage dance presentations for various forms of entertainment, collaborating with cast members and choreographing for individuals and groups, while auditioning performers and monitoring trends in dance.
Teach, train, and exercise to maintain high levels of technical proficiency, while seeking influences from other art forms and refining or modifying dance steps in collaboration with choreographers.
Perform classical, modern, or acrobatic dances in productions, expressing stories, rhythm, and sound, as well as coordinating with partners or dance ensembles.
Design sets, lighting, costumes, and other artistic elements for productions in collaboration with other artists and performers, ensuring aesthetic coherence in performances.
Attend auditions for dance roles and dance companies, and review and practice required dance moves for specific roles.
Monitor the field of dance to remain aware of current trends and innovations, while keeping records of client needs and preferences in instructional settings.
Demonstrate techniques and ideas to dancers, while advising them on correct methods to help prevent injuries and promote safe dance practices.
Study production information and scripts to determine how to translate ideas and moods into dance movements, while reading and analyzing story lines and musical scores.
Perform in dance-related productions, if required, and carry out related tasks to contribute to the overall production process.
Restage traditional dances to develop new interpretations and collaborate with dance companies to keep their repertoires fresh and relevant.
Attend costume fittings and makeup calls associated with dance performances to ensure alignment with production requirements.
Coordinate dancing with that of partners or dance ensembles, experimenting with different types of dancers and placements to refine performances.
No relevant tasks.

Organize, lead, and promote interest in recreational activities, such as arts, crafts, sports, games, camping, and hobbies, and explain principles, techniques, and safety procedures to participants while demonstrating use of materials and equipment.

Supervise and coordinate activities of dining room staff to ensure that patrons receive prompt and courteous service, and maintain contact with kitchen staff, management, serving staff, and customers to address dining details and concerns.

Develop program plans for individuals or assist in plan development; communicate with other staff to resolve problems with individual students and evaluate recreation areas, facilities, and services to ensure effectiveness.

Greet new arrivals to activities, introducing them to other participants, explaining facility rules, and encouraging participation; supervise and train staff to promote a smooth operation of recreational programs.
Direct special activities or events, such as aquatics or performing arts, and provide entertainment, setting up related decorations and equipment to enhance the experience.
Inspect dining and serving areas for cleanliness and proper setup; answer patron inquiries and ensure satisfaction with food and service while responding to complaints.
Order or requisition supplies for facilities and maintain inventory to ensure adequate resources for activities.
Manage the daily operations of recreational facilities and enforce rules and regulations to ensure a safe and orderly environment for participants.
Compile information on activities and the quantities of supplies used to prepare reports, and conduct evaluations to assist in future planning.
Communicate regularly with agency personnel, community organizations, and other professional staff to plan balanced recreational programs catering to the interests and needs of participants.
Make regular rounds to ensure that residents and areas are safe and secure; observe participants to detect and report any unusual behavior.
Collaborate with counselors and other staff to develop and coordinate educational programs addressing the needs of individuals or groups; mediate interpersonal problems as necessary.

Provide assistance and information to patrons, such as giving directions, handling inquiries, and explaining costs, availability, policies, and procedures of facilities. Assist patrons with special needs, such as helping those with wheelchairs, and address customer complaints or concerns. Maintain order and ensure adherence to safety rules at facilities or events.

Perform cleaning and maintenance tasks, such as cleaning facilities, maintaining a lost-and-found collection, and keeping storage areas and carts well-stocked and tidy. Monitor and inspect equipment to ensure it is functioning properly and meets cleanliness and safety standards. Mixing cleaning solutions and using equipment for thorough cleaning when necessary.

Sell or collect tickets, passes, or memberships from patrons at entertainment events. Issue ticket stubs, take numbered tags from customers, and give patrons matching tags for finding their vehicles. Greet patrons attending events and assist with locating their seats, including lighting the way if necessary.

Assist with the management and operation of event-related equipment and facilities, including setting up, adjusting, and maintaining projection equipment. Operate controls to regulate sound and image quality during performances and ensure that standards are met during multi-theater showings.

Perform a variety of supplemental tasks, including observing and reporting safety hazards, managing inventories, operating refreshment stands, or directing patrons as necessary. Maintain a safe environment by escalating issues to supervisors and ensuring adherence to safety protocols during events.
[Removed] Turn in receipts and money received from deliveries.
[Removed] Maintain records, such as vehicle logs, records of cargo, or billing statements, in accordance with regulations.
[Removed] Drive ambulances or assist ambulance drivers in transporting sick, injured, or convalescent persons.
[Removed] Place patients on stretchers, and load stretchers into ambulances, usually with assistance from other attendants.
[Removed] Clean facilities such as floors or locker rooms.
[Removed] Read maps and follow written or verbal geographic directions.
[Removed] Report delays, accidents, or other traffic and transportation situations to bases or other vehicles, using telephones or mobile two-way radios.
Provide dressing assistance to cast members or assign cast dressers to assist specific cast members with costume changes, and create worksheets for dressing lists, show notes, or costume checks.
Monitor compliance with standard operating procedures for loss prevention, physical security, or risk management, and implement or monitor processes to reduce property or financial losses.
Distribute costumes or related equipment, keep records of item status, and inventory stock to determine types or conditions of available costuming.
Return borrowed or rented items when productions are complete, and clean and press costumes before and after performances, performing any minor repairs as needed.
Direct the work of wardrobe crews during dress rehearsals or performances, and examine costume fit on cast members and sketch or write notes for alterations.

Review scripts or other production information to determine a story's locale or period, as well as the number of characters and required costumes, and study books, pictures, or examples of period clothing to determine styles worn during specific periods in history.

Collaborate with production designers, costume designers, or other production staff to discuss and execute costume design details, and design or construct costumes or send them to tailors for major repairs or alterations.

Purchase, rent, or requisition costumes or other wardrobe necessities, and monitor, maintain, or secure inventories of costumes, wigs, or makeup, providing keys or access to assigned directors, costume designers, or wardrobe supervisors.
Promote or provide hunter or trapper safety training, and protect and preserve native wildlife, plants, or ecosystems.
Participate in the hiring, training, scheduling, or supervision of alteration workers.
Schedule appointments and maintain and update appointment calendars.

Transmit information or documents to customers, using computer, mail, or facsimile machine, and perform administrative support tasks, such as proofreading, transcribing handwritten information, or operating calculators or computers to work with pay records, invoices, balance sheets, or other documents.
Analyze data to determine answers to questions from customers or members of the public.
Clean school bus interiors by picking up waste, wiping down windows, or vacuuming, and operate a wheelchair lift to load or unload wheelchairs.
Educate the public about animal welfare and animal control laws and regulations.
Inspect recreational facilities for cleanliness and ensure safety hazards are addressed during recreational equipment inspections.
Respond to and investigate reports of animal attacks, animal cruelty, and other incidents, including examining animals for injuries or malnutrition and collecting evidence.
Capture and remove stray, uncontrolled, or abused animals from undesirable conditions, arranging for medical treatment as necessary and adopting out unclaimed animals.
Write reports of activities, document observations made while on patrol, and maintain files related to animal control operations.
Train security personnel on protective procedures and first aid, as well as monitor their adherence to departmental policies and standards.
Monitor and direct traffic flow in order to ensure safe pedestrian movement at crossings and points of interest, using appropriate signals and signage.
Collect information and issue citations related to parking violations, including keeping records of enforcement activities and handling inquiries from the public.
Provide assistance to the public by answering inquiries about regulations and guiding them to services, ensuring a clear understanding of rules.
Perform cleaning and maintenance duties for various facilities, ensuring safe and hygienic environments in recreational or animal shelter contexts.
Provide passengers with assistance entering and exiting vehicles, and help them with any luggage.
Supply guests or travelers with directions, travel information, and other information, such as available services and points of interest.
Greet incoming guests and escort them to their rooms.
Deliver messages and items, such as newspapers, documents, and packages, between establishment departments and to other establishments and private homes.
Maintain clean lobbies or entrance areas for travelers or guests.
Prepare and submit reports that may include the number of passengers or trips, hours worked, mileage driven, fuel consumed, or fares received.
Perform routine maintenance on delivery vehicles, such as monitoring fluid levels and replenishing fuel.
Monitor activities to ensure adherence to rules and safety procedures, or arrange for the removal of unruly patrons.
Provide information about facilities, entertainment options, and rules and regulations.
Collect and record data associated with operating geothermal power plants or well fields.
Perform minor vehicle repairs, such as cleaning spark plugs, or take vehicles to mechanics for servicing.
Clean tracks or clear ice or snow from tracks or switch boxes.
Perform preventative maintenance on vehicles and heavy equipment.
Drive heavy equipment and vehicles with adjustable attachments to sweep debris from paved surfaces, mow grass and weeds, remove snow and ice, and spread salt and sand.
Inspect equipment to detect wear and damage and perform minor repairs, adjustments, or maintenance tasks, such as oiling parts.
Sell tickets and collect fees from customers.
Perform general office or clerical work, such as filing materials, operating duplicating machines, or running errands.
Inspect work areas or operating equipment to ensure conformance to established standards in areas such as cleanliness or maintenance.
Maintain inventories of equipment, storing and retrieving items and assembling and disassembling equipment as necessary.
Record details of attendance, sales, receipts, reservations, or repair activities.
Resolve customer complaints regarding worker performance or services rendered.
Participate in continuing education to stay abreast of industry trends and developments.
Recruit and hire staff members.
Requisition necessary supplies, equipment, or services.
Confer with customers, supervisors, contractors, or other personnel to exchange information or to resolve problems.
Direct or coordinate the activities of workers in recreational or entertainment activities.
Explain and demonstrate work tasks to new workers or assign training tasks to experienced workers.
Monitor field work to ensure proper performance and use of materials, and enforce safety rules and regulations.
